title: Agencies request comment on proposals to modernize the regulatory capital framework and maintain the strength of the banking system
summary: The FDIC, Federal Reserve Board, and OCC jointly requested comment on three proposals to modernize the regulatory capital framework for banks of all sizes. The proposals would streamline capital requirements and better align regulatory capital with risk while maintaining safety and soundness. The agencies anticipate modest capital reductions for large banks and moderate reductions for smaller banks, though capital levels would remain substantially higher than pre-financial crisis levels.
key_points: Three joint proposals aim to modernize the regulatory capital framework for banks of all sizes, with comment deadline of June 18, 2026
Proposal 1 (largest banks): enhances risk sensitivity, reduces burden, implements final Basel III components, and streamlines to one set of calculations for risk-based capital compliance
Proposal 1 includes improved calibration for credit, market, and operational risks; market risk framework applies only to banks with significant trading activity
Proposal 2 (all but largest banks): better aligns capital requirements for traditional lending activities, modifies mortgage servicing and originating capital requirements to reduce disincentives
Proposal 3 (Federal Reserve only): improves systemic risk measurement for determining additional capital requirements on the largest and most complex banks
Proposal 2 would require certain large banks, after a transition period, to reflect unrealized gains and losses on certain securities in regulatory capital levels
why_it_matters: These proposals represent the most significant restructuring of bank capital rules since Basel III implementation. Changes could affect risk appetites, lending costs, mortgage activity, and competitive dynamics between large and small banks. Market participants need to understand which proposal applies to institutions they monitor and how capital levels may shift.
market_structure_context: The proposals modify regulatory capital requirements across bank tiers, affecting how banks calculate risk-weighted assets, hold capital against mortgage servicing, and are subject to systemic risk surcharges. Changes to unrealized gains/losses treatment and the single-calculation framework for large banks represent meaningful structural shifts to bank balance sheet management requirements.
notes: Source is a substantive regulatory proposal press release from authoritative banking regulators. Three distinct proposals are detailed with clear applicability criteria by bank size. No specific numerical estimates of capital reduction are provided in the body text—only directional language ('modestly' for large banks, 'moderately' for smaller banks). Published 12 days before TODAY, making this a recent document. Multiple supporting documents referenced (fact sheet, board memo, data files) but not included in source body.
